page: OVHcloud r2-60 is a memory-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 Intel Core Processor (Haswell, no TSX) running at 2.2 GHz with 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs, meaning hyperthreading is disabled. The hardware configuration includes 60.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 15.0 GB per core. For local storage, the instance is equipped with a 100 GB SSD. It also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data indicates that the CPU performance is relatively modest. The stress-ng single-core test places the processor in the bottom 10%, while multi-core performance and Geekbench scores fall into the bottom 25%. Memory performance is mixed; while cached small-block read bandwidth is average, uncached large-block read bandwidth is poor, and write bandwidth is weak. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput also rank in the bottom 25%. However, the instance maintains average performance for text generation on small language models with 135M parameters.

Given the high memory density of 15.0 GB per core paired with older Intel Haswell architecture, this instance offers a cost-effective option for memory-bound workloads that do not require high-performance compute. The dedicated core allocation ensures predictable, albeit limited, CPU performance. This profile is suitable for hosting medium-sized caching layers, memory-intensive database replicas, or lightweight background workers that require a large memory footprint but have low CPU utilization.
description: The OVHcloud r2-60 is a memory-optimized virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It is configured with an x86_64 Intel Core Processor (Haswell, no TSX) featuring 4 dedicated physical cores, 4 vCPUs, and a clock speed of 2.2 GHz. The instance is equipped with 60.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high resource density of 15.0 GB per core, alongside a 100 GB local SSD. Benchmark results show modest CPU capabilities, with single-core and multi-core performance ranking in the lower percentiles. However, cached memory bandwidth and small-model text generation speeds remain average. This hardware profile provides a cost-efficient solution for memory-intensive workloads with low CPU demands, such as caching layers, database replicas, and memory-bound background processing.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring a high memory-to-core ratio and dedicated resources for memory-bound workloads with moderate compute needs.
